meta.notes: Tile IDs are 0-based, row-major: id = row * 12 + col. Verified entries sourced from AGENT-LEARNINGS.md and TILE-BUILDING-RULES.md.
meta.description: Deep semantic tile catalog for LLM-driven map generation. Each tile has visual descriptions, edge compatibility (Wang-tile style), semantic roles, and placement rules.

grass.description: Open green space — primary ground fill
rules: Never >4 same tile in a row
Cluster variants in organic blobs, not checkerboard

path.description: Dirt walkway connecting buildings and map features
rules: Must connect to every building door
Use edge tiles on grass-path boundaries

building-zone.description: Atomic building footprint — placed as complete template
rules: Door must face path within 2 tiles

fence-zone.description: Fence row below a building, with gate aligned to door
rules: Gate (-1) aligned with door column above
Placed 1 row below building wall row

water.description: Pond or water feature with proper edge tiles

forest.description: Dense tree border along map edges
rules: Add understory (ferns, small trees) at base

tree-cluster.description: Interior grove of 2-5 trees within the map

garden.description: Yard/buffer zone around buildings with flowers and bushes
rules: Placed within 3-4 tiles of parent building

decoration.description: Functional objects placed at points of interest
rules: Well at village center or path intersection

village-square.description: Central open area where paths converge

collectible-zone.description: Areas where quest items / pickups are placed

tiles.name: Sparkle Star
tiles.visual: Yellow 4-pointed sparkle/star on transparent bg — decorative overlay

placementRules.notes: Placement depends on map context.

tiles.name: Dark Wood Bench
tiles.visual: Dark wood bench (side view) — WARNING: reads as dark bomb at 16px. DEPRECATED.

placementRules.notes: Placement depends on map context.

tiles.neverUseReason: Too dark at 16px, reads as 'dark bomb' to young players. Avoid in village levels.

tiles.name: Gray Stone / Crate
tiles.visual: Gray stone/crate — WARNING: dark rounded shape reads as bomb. DEPRECATED. Use 107.

placementRules.notes: Placement depends on map context.

tiles.neverUseReason: Dark rounded shape reads as 'bomb' at small sizes. Use tile 107 (barrel) instead.

compositeObjects.name: Small House (3-wide, red roof, wood walls)

placementRules.notes: Door (tile 74) must face a path within 1-3 tiles. Fence row below with gap at door.

compositeObjects.name: Medium House (4-wide, red roof, wood walls)

placementRules.notes: Window tile 75 left of door. Minimum 3-tile yard around building.

compositeObjects.name: Large House (5-wide, red roof, wood walls)

placementRules.notes: Symmetric layout: window-door-window. Per TILE-BUILDING-RULES template.

compositeObjects.name: Stone Shop (3-wide, red roof, dark stone walls)

placementRules.notes: Uses dark stone walls (84-87) for shops/important buildings. Barrels (107) nearby.

compositeObjects.name: Large Stone Shop (5-wide, red roof, dark stone walls)

placementRules.notes: Large stone shop with windows on both sides of door.
Canopy (4,5) on FOREGROUND layer, trunks (12,13) on OBJECTS layer. Canopy row ABOVE trunk row. Never place trunk at row 0.
Autumn canopy (7,8) on FOREGROUND layer, trunks (24,25) on OBJECTS layer. Same depth rules as green tree.
Pine top (10) on FOREGROUND, trunk (22) on OBJECTS. 1-tile wide.
Unverified dense tree variant. Same structure as pine tree.
Single-tile standalone tree on OBJECTS layer.
Single-tile tree with visible trunk on OBJECTS layer.
Desert decoration. Left (20) and right (21) must be adjacent.
All 9 edge tiles required. Never adjacent to buildings (2+ tiles gap). Surround with flower tiles, bushes on 1-2 sides. Can extend center with more 122 tiles and edge repeats for larger ponds.
Well top (92) directly above well base (104). Place at path intersections or square centers.

compositeObjects.name: White Fence Segment (variable width with gate gap)

placementRules.notes: Gap (-1 = empty) aligned with building door above. 96=left end, 97=repeatable middle, 98=right end. Width varies. 108=vertical post for corners.
Brown wood fence. 99=left end, 100=repeatable middle, 101=right end.
Closed (128) and open (129) are animation pair. Place on OBJECTS layer.

compositeObjects.name: Brick Wall Building with Red Roof (3-wide)

terrain.description: Ground fill tiles (grass, sand, stone floor)

decoration.description: Placed objects (well, barrel, signs, items, NPCs)

animation.description: Tiles that are animation frame pairs

criticalReminders: Tile 14 = peach block, DEPRECATED for village use
Tile 105 = bench, DEPRECATED (looks like bomb at 16px)
Tile 106 = crate, DEPRECATED (looks like bomb at 16px)
Tile 116 = signpost, DEPRECATED (looks like weapon at 16px)
Use tile 93 for lanterns instead of 116
Use tile 107 for barrels instead of 106
Canopy is ALWAYS above trunk, never reversed
Autumn tree trunks are 24/25 (per ASSET-VISUAL-AUDIT), not 12/13

deprecatedTiles.name: Peach/Cream Block
deprecatedTiles.reason: Renders as peach/cream block, NOT a bush. Previously misidentified. Use tile 28 for bushes instead.

deprecatedTiles.name: Dark Wood Bench
deprecatedTiles.reason: Too dark at 16px, reads as 'dark bomb' to young players. Avoid in village levels.

deprecatedTiles.name: Gray Stone / Crate
deprecatedTiles.reason: Dark rounded shape reads as 'bomb' at small sizes. Use tile 107 (barrel) instead.

deprecatedTiles.name: Sign/Lamp Post
deprecatedTiles.reason: Cross/trident shape reads as 'fork/weapon' at small sizes. Use tile 93 (lantern) instead.
